title: tridax angustifolia subsp. stuebelii
intro: Tridax angustifolia subsp. stuebelii, also known as the false daisy or tridax, is a plant species native to South America. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The plant has been found to have numerous health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and protecting against oxidative stress. The leaves of the Tridax angustifolia subsp. stuebelii can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, or dried and used as a powder for medicinal purposes.
